heading: Definitions
label: 14101 Definitions
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised section 14101Source: International Convention on Tonnage Measurement of Ships.Section 14101 contains definitions that are limited to Part J—Measurement of Vessels. The source of these definitions is the International Convention on Tonnage Measurement of Ships in articles 2 and 4.

Amendments2010—Par. (4). , substituted “that engages” for “engaged” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 111–281, § 303(a)(1)Par. (4)(A). , substituted “that arrives” for “arriving”.Pub. (4)(C). , substituted “that departs” for “departing”.Pub. L. 111–281, § 303(a)(4)Par. (4)(D). , substituted “that makes” for “making”.Pub. L. 111–281, § 303(a)(5)

children.text: “Convention” means the International Convention on Tonnage Measurement of Ships, 1969.
“existing vessel” means a vessel the keel of which was laid or that was at a similar stage of construction before .July 18, 1982

children.label: i
children.text: a rhumb line drawn from Cap des Rosiers to West Point, Anticosti Island; and

children.label: ii
children.text: on the north side of Anticosti Island, the meridian of longitude 63 degrees west.

children.label: A
children.text: that arrives at a place under the jurisdiction of the United States from a place in a foreign country;

children.label: B
children.text: that makes a voyage between places outside the United States;

children.label: C
children.text: that departs from a place under the jurisdiction of the United States for a place in a foreign country; or

children.label: D
children.text: that makes a voyage between a place within a territory or possession of the United States and another place under the jurisdiction of the United States not within that territory or possession.
